message:
feat(ondisk,inode): preserve i_version and i_version_hi across inode writes

Added `version` (offset 0x24, i_osd1/l_i_version) and `version_hi`
(offset 0x98, i_version_hi) fields to Ext4Inode. These form the 64-bit
NFS change attribute used by NFSv4 and statx(2) for cache coherence.

Previously, every inode write silently zeroed both fields because they
were not modeled in the struct, causing NFS clients to incorrectly
believe their caches were valid after server-side modifications.

Both fields are parsed during inode read and serialized during write,
ensuring round-trip preservation. New inodes initialize both to 0.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

             xattr_ibody: Vec::new(),

@@ -158,6 +158,8 @@ fn serialize_inode(inode: &Ext4Inode, inode_size: usize) -> Vec<u8> {
     buf[0x1C..0x20].copy_from_slice(&(inode.blocks as u32).to_le_bytes());
     // Flags (0x20).
     buf[0x20..0x24].copy_from_slice(&inode.flags.to_le_bytes());
+    // i_osd1 / l_i_version (0x24) — NFS change attribute low 32 bits.
+    buf[0x24..0x28].copy_from_slice(&inode.version.to_le_bytes());
     // i_block / extent bytes (0x28, 60 bytes).
     let copy_len = inode.extent_bytes.len().min(60);
     buf[0x28..0x28 + copy_len].copy_from_slice(&inode.extent_bytes[..copy_len]);
@@ -197,6 +199,10 @@ fn serialize_inode(inode: &Ext4Inode, inode_size: usize) -> Vec<u8> {
             buf[0x90..0x94].copy_from_slice(&inode.crtime.to_le_bytes());
             buf[0x94..0x98].copy_from_slice(&inode.crtime_extra.to_le_bytes());
         }
+        if inode_size >= 0x9C {
+            // i_version_hi (0x98) — NFS change attribute high 32 bits.
+            buf[0x98..0x9C].copy_from_slice(&inode.version_hi.to_le_bytes());
+        }
         if inode_size >= 0xA0 {
             buf[0x9C..0xA0].copy_from_slice(&inode.projid.to_le_bytes());
         }
